hero.title: The perfect ORM for hapi developers
hero.description: Query data from MySQL, PostgreSQL & SQL Server databases in hapi apps with Prisma – a better ORM for JavaScript and TypeScript.

cards.title: Smooth integration
cards.description: Prisma fits perfectly well into the flexible architecture of hapi, no matter if you're building REST or GraphQL APIs.

cards.title: Higher productivity
cards.description: Prisma's gives you autocompletion for database queries, great developer experience and full type safety.

cards.title: Type-safe database client
cards.description: Prisma Client ensures fully type-safe database queries with benefits like autocompletion - even in JavaScript.

cards.title: Intuitive data modeling
cards.description: Prisma's declarative modeling language is simple and lets you intuitively describe your database schema.

cards.title: Easy database migrations
cards.description: Generate predictable and customizable SQL migrations from the declarative Prisma schema.

cards.title: Designed for building APIs
cards.description: Prisma Client reduces boilerplates by providing queries for common API features (e.g. pagination, filters, ...).

how.title: How Prisma and hapi fit together
how.description: Prisma is a next-generation ORM that's used to query your database in a hapi app. You can use it as an alternative to writing plain SQL queries, to using query builders like knex.js or to traditional ORMs like TypeORM, MikroORM and Sequelize.

body.content:
prismaPlugin

A prismaPlugin is the foundation for the domain- or model-specific plugins. The PrismaClient instance it contains provides the database interface to the rest of the application.

While Prisma works great with hapi, you can use it with any other web framework like koa.js, Fastify or FeathersJS as well.

usersPlugin

Once your prismaPlugin is ready, you can use it to query your database inside other plugins, for example to query your models.

In this case, it's demonstrated how a new user record can be created via the plugin.

Prisma schema

The Prisma schema uses Prisma's declarative modeling language to define your database schema. It makes data modeling easy and intuitive, especially when it comes to modeling relations.

cards.title: Building a modern backend with TypeScript, PostgreSQL & Prisma
cards.description: A tutorial series for building a modern backend with hapi and Prisma.

cards.title: REST API starter kit
cards.description: A ready-to-run example project for a REST API with a SQLite database.

cards.title: GraphQL API starter kit
cards.description: A ready-to-run example project for a GraphQL API with a SQLite database.
